concepts.title: Phase space and the cotangent bundle
concepts.blurb: Configuration space $Q$ records positions; physically meaningful states pair positions with momenta, so the natural arena is the cotangent bundle $T^*Q$. The tautological 1-form $\theta = \sum p_i\,dq^i$ has exterior derivative $\omega = -d\theta = \sum dq^i\wedge dp_i$, the canonical symplectic form. Canonical coordinates $(q^i,p_i)$ are not just convenience — they're the Darboux coordinates already built into $T^*Q$.

concepts.title: Hamilton's equations
concepts.blurb: A Hamiltonian $H\colon T^*Q\to\mathbb{R}$ generates evolution by $\dot q^i = \partial H/\partial p_i,\ \dot p_i = -\partial H/\partial q^i$. These are exactly the integral curves of the Hamiltonian vector field $X_H$ defined by $\iota_{X_H}\omega = dH$. Energy conservation $\dot H = 0$ and Liouville's theorem (volume preservation) follow at once from $\mathcal{L}_{X_H}\omega = 0$.

concepts.title: Canonical transformations and Hamilton–Jacobi
concepts.blurb: A diffeomorphism $\varphi\colon T^*Q\to T^*Q$ is canonical (a symplectomorphism) when $\varphi^*\omega = \omega$. Locally each is encoded by a generating function $S(q,Q)$: $p_i = \partial S/\partial q^i,\ P_i = -\partial S/\partial Q^i$. Choosing $S$ so the new Hamiltonian vanishes gives the Hamilton–Jacobi equation $\partial S/\partial t + H(q,\partial S/\partial q) = 0$ — solving it integrates the dynamics.

concepts.title: Symmetries and Noether's theorem
concepts.blurb: If $f\in C^\infty(T^*Q)$ Poisson-commutes with $H$, then $f$ is conserved along the flow: $\dot f = \{f,H\} = 0$. Conversely every continuous symmetry of $H$ has a moment map $f$ generating it. Translations $\to$ linear momentum, rotations $\to$ angular momentum, time-translation $\to$ energy. Noether's theorem in Hamiltonian dress: symmetries and conserved quantities are the same data.

concepts.title: Integrable systems and KAM
concepts.blurb: Liouville–Arnold: if $T^*Q$ has $n$ functionally independent first integrals $f_1=H,f_2,\dots,f_n$ in involution ($\{f_i,f_j\}=0$), each compact regular level set is a torus $T^n$ and there exist action-angle coordinates $(I,\theta)$ in which $\dot I = 0,\ \dot\theta = \partial H/\partial I$. KAM: under a small non-degenerate perturbation of an integrable $H$, most invariant tori survive (those with sufficiently irrational frequency vectors).

concepts.title: Worked examples
concepts.blurb: The harmonic oscillator $H=\tfrac12(p^2+\omega^2 q^2)$ is the cleanest integrable system; its phase portrait is concentric ellipses. The Kepler problem $H=\tfrac{|p|^2}{2m}-\tfrac{k}{|q|}$ is super-integrable — angular momentum and the Laplace–Runge–Lenz vector pin orbits to closed conics. The free rigid body, geodesic flow on a Riemannian manifold, and convex billiards round out the canonical examples; the three-body problem is famously not integrable.
